secret_in_bundle_scan.verdict: PASS - no secret values present in any shipped frontend file (P0 clear)

controls: http(s) scheme only (blocks file://, gopher://, etc.)
encoded/decimal IPs (e.g. http://2130706433/) are resolved then blocked
ssrf.fetch_path: validated user URL is handed to Anakin Universal Scraper (anakin_client.scrape -> api.anakin.io); the target page is fetched by Anakin, not by the app. The app's own httpx call to api.anakin.io does not follow redirects.

residual_gaps.note: validate_public_url validates the initial URL only. Redirect-following and DNS-rebinding happen on Anakin's side; not locally reachable to the app's metadata endpoint. No app-local SSRF via redirects for user URLs.

scraper_service.py trend fetchers use httpx follow_redirects=True but only against fixed trusted hosts (reddit.com, news.google.com, googleapis.com, newsapi.org) - not user-controlled, not an SSRF vector.
No explicit port allowlist; a public IP on a non-standard port could be requested. Mitigated because Anakin performs the fetch.

body_size.gap: checks Content-Length header only; a chunked request without Content-Length bypasses the cap (low)

input_and_size_limits.pydantic_field_limits: present: creator<=120, topic<=200, website<=2000, product/goal<=500, campaign name<=200, impressions ge0 le2e9, compare creators 2..3

path_traversal.control: persistence/repository.py LocalRepository._file() strips to [alnum-_] only; IDs are UUIDs

filename_sanitization.frontend_downloads: data: URIs with fixed names (voiceover.mp3, scene-N.png) - safe

ffmpeg_arg_safety.finding: no subprocess/Popen/os.system/shell=True in backend; ffmpeg only referenced via shutil.which() for capability detection (capability_registry.py). No arg-injection surface found in scanned code.

html_escaping.control: formatters.js escapeHtml used pervasively; renderMarkdown escapes before inline formatting; markdown link regex allows http(s) only (no javascript: URIs)
html_escaping.verdict: PASS - no unescaped user data paths found in views

log_redaction.control: security.py mask_secret(); startup print emits only OK/not-configured booleans, no secret values

cors.config: main.py CORSMiddleware allow_origins=cors_origins() (FRONTEND_ORIGINS env, default localhost list), allow_credentials=False, methods=[GET,POST,PUT,OPTIONS], headers=[Content-Type]
cors.action_required: set FRONTEND_ORIGINS to the deployed origin in production (or rely on same-origin CloudFront proxy where CORS is not exercised)

rate_limiting.control: security.py RateLimiter in-memory sliding window (research 10/60s, generation 20/60s)

gap.note: per-process in-memory - does not hold across multiple workers/instances (ECS Fargate / App Runner multi-instance). Abuse control weakens horizontally.
